item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get the feeling that the oil inside of her is the remains of his wife

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

It was really hard for me to decide how to vote on this one since the Special Containment Procedure made me want to stop reading especially the line "nearing the third decade of life." (I cringed), Letting it as in her late 20's would have worked better. It would also have been great to know SCP-032's height since no image was provided (It be great if there was one).

However, the interviews really made the piece better even if they were a little bit overdramatic. The link to 1440 and the general mystery around the brotherhood is what made me able to get through the whole file.

So ya the story brought on by the interview is the reason I left a +1 mostly since it seems to open the door to alot of narratives. PLEASE work on the special containment procedures tho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAA I can not *believe* I only just now found this one. I love it. I love it so bad omg.

"I used to be his wife." I came so close to making a scene in class I freaked out so hard. That's how you drop a wham line, friends.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the line [SCP-032 possesses extreme adverse effects to any biological entity in its close vicinity not created,] should be changed to [SCP-032 has extreme adverse effects to any biological entity in its proximity that is not created,]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like 032, I love it even more now that I know there's more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didnt realize that 1440 has a companion piece???!! Just hrnggffhghhhhh, this is fantastic I am vibrating in joy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't read the related reading material, so I'd like to say that this stands wonderfully on its own. I hope when I get around to reading more, it will be worth giving up the mystery. I especially enjoy how you got across the sheer sadisticness of this SCP's creators though the interviews.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The vote is fine, but I would like to point out that the creature being left in Foundation custody is a vital component of its function in this particular article, as the logs clarify.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -1. This didn't grab me at all (which, for a otherwise well-crafted article would be a neutral vote), but things what turn themselves in or things what are found by an agent at random are a personal peeve of mine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, those three brothers from 1440 are absolute assholes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a diabolically clever and overall fantastic compliment to 1440, and yet it stands perfectly well on its own. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is certainly much better for the slot. *shudder*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ability to produce photosynthesis

Shouldn't this be just "ability to photosynthesize"?

As for the rest of the article, it has hints of interesting things going on and flows nicely but as someone who has not read the related material this didn't tell a complete enough story by itself for me to feel justified upvoting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels way too supplementary to 1440, to me at least. The connection didn't really do anything for me, and that's the only reason I can find to care about this in the end.

Also, there seems to be a double downvote from DrSolorum. Might wanna ask them to fix that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No wonder I don't remember having read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At any rate, I like this. The fact that it's set in Slovakia is just a cherry on top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Caucasian" is an outmoded and unscientific term, even though it is sometimes incorrectly used in real-life scientific papers. You're likely looking for "of European descent". Due to the real life use of this, you can get away with it, but it's still deeply silly.

Aww, it's a wonderful term to troll all the eastern europeans like me with.
I remember when I was in early teens, its use led me to believe that a number of comic characters (most notably Wally from Dilbert) were from actual Caucasus region.
Also, allows for Transcaucasian to be a marvellous IRL pun. (Though, the SSR in question ceased to exist a good while ago)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright.

Some nitpicks:

  • The Special Containment Procedures are a wall-of-text-y paragraph that could stand to be broken up. Actually, breaking up the later paragraphs would help readability too.
  • "Caucasian" is an outmoded and unscientific term, even though it is sometimes incorrectly used in real-life scientific papers. You're likely looking for "of European descent". Due to the real life use of this, you can get away with it, but it's still deeply silly.

As for the rest… it's okay. It deals in tropes that feel fairly cliche, and the conversations are really repetitive; you should at least cut 'em down. [edit: though I did not find them unrealistically overdramatic.] But the ending of each conversation has genuine impact. So I'm left feeling partially intrigued and partially feeling "this is tiresome". I'm not sure where that puts my vote.

Apologies that I don't really have more specific suggestions; if I think of any, I'll try to, er, suggest them.

EDIT: on re-read this does work better for me, so +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was those flower bulbs with fairies inside of them that were sad

Found a copy of the article by googling it, here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hint: it's related to this and this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think I really get it. The dialogue was a bit too serious and dramatic for me, as well. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, why they gotta hate on 1440 like that. I have to say, I wasn't too enthralled by this until the end of the first interview, at which point I was going "I sure hope the author has an explanation for all this in the comments!" And, well, you didn't, but there is one, so I'm satisfied.

Bee tee dubs: what was the original 032?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was a "personal" that should be "personnel". Now it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay you posted it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I'm not entirely sure this is worthy of those double digits. I mean, don't we have some other things like her. Well, let's just read the interview logs… Damn. He did it again."

Even if I didn't know 1440 already, this would still be masterful characterization.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-032: I used to be his wife.

SCP-032: Do you know what it feels like, to be made as a mockery? In every line of that smooth, silent face, I saw a twisted reflection in my own. Fragrant skin to molded plastic, soft hair to synthetic fiber, blood to oil. Soul to nothing at all.

SCP-032: He never won.

/incoherent screech

Having read and adored both 1440 and Thrice, I think I need to lie down and contemplate life (and death, naturally. Perhaps all three of them.) for a little bit. Here, have an upvote, and may many more throw themselves upon this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your capability for character via dialogue makes me want to choke you again and again.

By that I mean, I really admire it and wish I had such skill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1440 is an awesome article, Dmatix. And so is this.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both to it and to the expanded back story presented in Thrice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohmygodohmygodohmygod
This is related to SCP-1440 isn't it
Oh my god
Yes
yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it as a draft love it now. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first SCP in quite some time. Its connection to some of my other works should be fairly apparent, but hopefully it stands on its own as well.
